skill.name: core-components
skill.description: Core component library and design system patterns for building consistent user interfaces.
skill.summary: Design system documentation for React Native core components with design tokens and layout patterns.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analyzer flagged 57 patterns (49 external_commands, 8 blocker) that are all false positives. The backtick detections are Markdown code fence delimiters (```tsx) used for syntax highlighting in documentation, not shell execution. No cryptographic code exists in this file. This is a documentation-only skill teaching React Native component library usage with design tokens. No security risks detected.

content.user_title: Build consistent UIs with core components
content.value_statement: Inconsistent styling and fragmented component usage create maintenance challenges. This skill provides a unified design system with tokens and patterns for cohesive interfaces.
actual_capabilities: Guide proper usage of design tokens for spacing, colors, and typography
Provide core component patterns including Box, HStack, VStack, Text, Button, Input, and Card
Demonstrate screen layout and form layout patterns
Show anti-patterns to avoid when building interfaces
Explain component prop patterns with token-based typing
limitations: Documentation focuses on a specific design system implementation
Requires existing core component library to be installed in the project
Patterns are optimized for React Native development
Does not cover platform-specific native component creation

use_cases.title: Frontend Developer Building New Features
use_cases.description: A developer needs to add a new settings screen with forms and lists. They use this skill to apply consistent spacing tokens, color tokens, and component patterns that match the existing design system.

use_cases.title: Team Standardizing UI Components
use_cases.description: A team notices inconsistent styling across their app. They use this skill to establish shared patterns for layouts, typography, and interactive elements using design tokens.

use_cases.title: Code Reviewer Enforcing Standards
use_cases.description: During code review, a developer identifies hard-coded values and raw platform components. They reference this skill to guide contributors toward proper token usage and core components.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Component Usage
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to create a card component with a title and content using the core component library with proper design tokens.

prompt_templates.title: Form Layout Pattern
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a login form with email and password inputs using VStack, Input components, and appropriate spacing tokens. Include a submit button.

prompt_templates.title: List Item with Avatar
prompt_templates.prompt: Build a list item component showing a user avatar, name, and subtitle with a chevron icon. Use HStack, proper gap tokens, and semantic color tokens for text hierarchy.

prompt_templates.title: Token Conversion Review
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this component code and identify any hard-coded values that should use design tokens. Show the corrected version with proper token usage for spacing, colors, and typography.

output_examples.input: Create a settings screen header with title and icon
output_examples.output: A ScreenHeader component with the title text using $textPrimary color and an icon positioned in an HStack with $3 gap, wrapped in a Box with $4 padding and $backgroundPrimary background.
A Button component with variant set to solid, isDisabled prop set to true, which automatically applies muted styling using $textTertiary for the label color.

best_practices: Always use design tokens ($4, $textPrimary) instead of hard-coded values (16, #333333)
Import from components/core instead of raw react-native primitives
Use HStack and VStack for layouts instead of manual flexbox configuration
anti_patterns: Using View and Text from react-native instead of Box and Text from core components
Hard-coding pixel values for spacing, colors, or typography
Creating inline style objects instead of using token-based props

faq.question: What are design tokens and why should I use them?
faq.answer: Design tokens are named values that represent design decisions like spacing, colors, and typography. Using tokens ensures consistency across your app and makes theme changes easier.

faq.question: How do I know which spacing token to use?
faq.answer: Reference the spacing token table: $1 is 4px, $2 is 8px, $3 is 12px, $4 is 16px, $6 is 24px, and $8 is 32px. Choose based on the visual density needed.

faq.question: Can I use core components with existing react-native code?
faq.answer: Yes, core components wrap react-native primitives. You can gradually migrate by replacing View with Box and Text with the core Text component.

faq.question: What if I need a value that is not in the token scale?
faq.answer: Extend the design token system rather than hard-coding. Add the new value to your theme configuration to maintain consistency.

faq.question: Are core components compatible with React Native Web?
faq.answer: Yes, the core component library is designed for cross-platform compatibility including iOS, Android, and Web targets.

faq.question: How do I handle component variants like button styles?
faq.answer: Use the variant prop with predefined values like solid, outline, ghost, or link. Each variant applies consistent styling for its intended use case.
